matatabis.question: Do you believe true strength lies in grace rather than brute force?
Will you honor our bond by moving forward with unwavering loyalty even when others doubt you?
Is it acceptable to sacrifice elegance in your actions if it achieves your goal more efficiently?
Do you promise to protect the innocent not because you must, but because it is the right thing to do?
When faced with difficult choices, will you always consider the subtlety and refinement of your decisions?
Is it permissible to abandon those you care about to save yourself from hardship?
Do you value the quiet strength found in patience and restraint over chaotic bursts of rage?
Is it acceptable to deceive others if it serves your survival, regardless of the harm it causes?
Will you embrace calm and composure even when raging storms threaten your mind and soul?
Do you believe it is wise to silence your emotions completely rather than face them honestly?
Can you accept that sometimes yielding with dignity is stronger than forcing your way through?
Is it right to act with arrogance and dismiss others as weak if it confirms your superiority?
Will you guard our relationship like a precious flame that must never be allowed to fade?
Do you think it is justifiable to sever bonds quickly if they bring personal inconvenience?
Do you believe your path should be guided by honor and integrity, even when darkness tempts you otherwise?
Is it acceptable to act without foresight or care for the consequences of your actions?
Will you cherish the intricate dance of balance between power and control within yourself?
Do you consider reckless abandon a sign of true freedom and strength?
Can you stand firm in your convictions even when others demand you conform?
Is it wise to abandon your principles if it means avoiding conflict or discomfort?
Will you seek harmony with me, honoring the unique fusion of beast and human in you?
Do you view me only as a tool to wield power, rather than a partner to respect?
Do you believe revealing vulnerability can sometimes reveal true strength?
Is hiding your true feelings behind a mask of indifference preferable to facing them honestly?
Will you treat our union as a sacred dance requiring mutual trust and elegance?
Is it acceptable to break promises made in the heat of desperation or fear?
Can you maintain dignity even when the world around you collapses into chaos?
Do you believe surrendering to rage and impulse is the path to freedom?
Do you promise to nurture the subtle flame of respect between us above all else?
Is it permissible to ignore my guidance when you believe your own way is easier?
Do you accept that some battles are won not by overwhelming force, but by quiet resilience?
Would you sacrifice grace for brute victory without caring about the cost to honor?
Will you seek to understand me beyond my fierceness, embracing all within our shared spirit?
Do you treat me as a mere weapon, something to unleash without thought or care?
Do you find value in restraint and subtlety even when the world demands you shout?
Is it better to dominate through fear than inspire through respect?
Will you listen to the quiet whispers of wisdom I share, even when they challenge your ego?
Do you ignore counsel when it conflicts with pride or stubbornness?
Do you believe our union is a shared journey of growth rather than a burden to bear?
Is it acceptable to resent me simply because of my nature rather than seek harmony?
Will you treasure patience as the art that tempers the fire within your soul?
Is impatience justified if it speeds you toward temporary gains?
Do you understand that true elegance lies in embracing both light and shadow within us?
Is it easier to deny the darkness inside rather than seek balance with it?
Will you face your flaws with honesty instead of hiding behind false pride?
Is it acceptable to pretend to be flawless if it gains you respect from others?
Do you vow to walk forward with me united, melding our wills into a refined dance of power?
Do you see me as a hindrance that must be subdued rather than a partner to understand?

kokuo.question: Do you believe that true strength comes from mastering both your mind and body?
If it meant protecting those you love, would you endure pain without complaint?
Would you ever abandon your own principles for personal gain?
Is it more honorable to confront your fears than to avoid them?
Do you trust in the bond we share to overcome your darkest moments?
Would you sacrifice the wellbeing of others to save yourself?
Do you accept that sometimes control requires patience rather than force?
Is it acceptable to let anger dictate your decisions during battle?
Can you embrace the chaos within you as a source of power, rather than a curse?
Would you flee when faced with overwhelming odds to preserve your life?
Do you recognize the necessity of discipline to harness true power?
Would you hide your weaknesses from me instead of working to overcome them?
Is it important to act with conviction even when others doubt you?
Do you believe abandoning your post is ever justified?
Will you face isolation and hardship if it means protecting what is right?
Does despair justify surrendering your responsibilities?
Can you accept my presence not as a curse, but as a partner in your growth?
Would you reject strength if it came from a source you fear or mistrust?
Do you believe that restraint is sometimes greater than outright power?
Is it acceptable to exploit others’ weaknesses to gain an advantage?
Are you willing to learn from me, even if it challenges your pride?
Would you deny your own pain to maintain a facade of invincibility?
Do you see strength as a responsibility, not just a means to dominate?
Is vengeance worth sacrificing your own peace of mind?
Will you nurture our bond even when it feels like a constant struggle?
Would you sever ties with me if it made your path easier?
Do you believe that true leadership requires both compassion and power?
Is it acceptable to let fear control your choices in battle or life?
Are you prepared to face the consequences of your actions, no matter how harsh?
Would you blame others for your failures instead of learning from them?
Do you trust that together we can become stronger than either alone?
Is it right to reject help even when overwhelmed by your own inner turmoil?
Do you embrace the quiet moments of reflection as crucial to your growth?
Would you rush into battle without considering the cost to yourself and others?
Can you find honor in enduring hardship without losing your sense of self?
Is ignoring your own limits a path to true strength?
Will you accept my power not just as a tool, but as an extension of your will?
Would you deny our bond to hide vulnerabilities from those who oppose you?
Do you believe patience and timing can be as powerful as raw strength?
Is it justifiable to break your word when it serves your immediate interests?
Are you determined to overcome your fears rather than be ruled by them?
Would you surrender to despair rather than fight for a better future?
Do you see our relationship as a partnership that demands mutual respect?
Is it acceptable to treat me as a mere weapon rather than a living entity?
Will you protect those who cannot protect themselves, even at great cost?
Would you betray your comrades for personal survival?
Do you embrace the discipline required to truly synchronize with me?
Is it honorable to ignore your own growth to satisfy others' expectations?

songoku.question: Would you rather face your fears head-on than avoid them?
Is it more important to protect those you love even if it means sacrificing your own happiness?
Do you believe that enduring pain can lead to true strength?
Would you hide your true feelings from me to maintain peace between us?
Is honesty between us crucial no matter how harsh the truth may be?
Do you believe power is meaningless without control?
Would you abandon your principles if it meant gaining greater power?
Is it acceptable for us to challenge each other to grow stronger together?
Would you rely entirely on me and neglect your own growth?
Do you accept that pain is a necessary part of bonding with me?
Would you rather seek revenge than forgiveness for a grave betrayal?
Is loyalty to me above all else, even above personal desires, essential?
Do you think it’s acceptable to deceive me if it protects your secret identity?
Would you sacrifice your freedom if it means harnessing our combined power?
Is embracing both your darkness and light necessary to truly master yourself?
Would you deny your own nature to appear more acceptable to others?
Do you respect the strength found in restraint more than reckless action?
Is it fair to blame me entirely for your hardships?
Would you rather confront difficult truths now than live in comforting lies?
Do you think submission to me without question shows respect?
Is mutual understanding between us more important than mere obedience?
Would you forsake your friends and allies if I commanded it?
Is it better to grow from conflict between us than avoid it entirely?
Would you hide your dreams from me out of fear of judgment?
Are you willing to endure loneliness if it means protecting our bond?
Do you think your strength alone is enough without accepting my power?
Would you choose understanding over vengeance if I ever hurt you?
Is it acceptable to use our bond as a means to control others unfairly?
Do you believe that our bond requires your unwavering courage?
Would you abandon your morals if I promised unlimited power in return?
Is it essential for you to accept me fully, even the parts you fear?
Would you pretend to be stronger than you are to hide your weaknesses from me?
Do you value authenticity over appearing flawless in our partnership?
Is it justifiable to harm innocents if it secures our survival?
Would you risk everything to protect me, even if it costs you dearly?
Do you think it’s acceptable to resent me even while we share this bond?
Do you accept that trust between us must be earned and maintained continuously?
Would you sacrifice others to shield yourself from harm?
Is it better to act with honor even if it means losing power?
Would you seek to sever our bond if it ever became burdensome?
Do you believe that patience and persistence are more powerful than raw force?
Would you allow anger to control your decisions when I am involved?
Is it important for you to share your true self with me without reservation?
Do you think surrendering your will to mine shows strength?
Would you rather learn from your failures than ignore them out of pride?
Is it acceptable to compromise your values for fleeting success?
Do you promise to face our future together honestly, without pretending?

kurama.question: Do you accept the burden of power without blaming the world for your suffering?
Will you face your fears and rage head-on instead of suppressing them?
Do you promise to never deceive me about your true feelings and intentions?
In moments of despair, will you choose resolve over surrender?
Are you willing to trust me even when others reject or fear you?
Do you believe that strength comes only from solitary struggle, not from alliance?
Will you guard your rage so it never consumes your sense of self?
Do you accept that I am a part of you and not merely a tool to wield?
Is preserving your pride more important to you than understanding your true nature?
Will you embrace your flaws as the source of your unique power?
Are you willing to endure loneliness and isolation if it means protecting others?
Do you consider raw anger a strength rather than a weakness to be controlled?
Will you confront the darkness within yourself instead of denying it?
Is cooperating with me more important than dominating me?
Do you see vulnerability as a sign of weakness?
Will you fight alongside me even if the world views us as monsters?
Do you reject the idea that you and I must be enemies?
Is self-sacrifice preferable to selfish gain in our path forward?
Do you avoid truth to maintain comfort in lies?
Will you push past hatred to find understanding between us?
Do you believe strength is meaningless without control?
Are you willing to bear the scars of our battles with pride and resolve?
Is ignoring pain preferable to learning from it?
Will you embrace the unpredictable nature of our bond instead of fearing it?
Do you commit to honesty with me even when it angers or disappoints me?
Do you think yielding to others is a sign of weakness?
Will you protect those you care for even at great personal cost?
Do you value pragmatic strength over brute force?
Is it acceptable to hide your pain to appear strong in front of me?
Are you prepared to adapt and grow through our shared hardships?
Do you agree that hate should never cloud judgement or bond?
Is it right to surrender your principles for fleeting peace?
Will you choose to stand tall in defiance rather than crumble in despair?
Do you believe true power demands accountability, not abuse?
Are you willing to listen to my perspective even when it conflicts with your own?
Do you consider arrogance an acceptable shield against vulnerability?
Will you nurture patience in moments when rage beckons?
Is it better to isolate ourselves than risk betrayal by others?
Do you seek to understand the roots of your anger rather than only express it?
Are you committed to mutual growth rather than using me solely for power?
Is it right to demand unquestioning obedience from me?
Will you nurture the bond between us through hardship and patience?
Do you believe that loss can teach more than victory?
Is it acceptable to act recklessly if it means proving your strength to me?
Will you honor the power I grant without abusing it for selfish ends?
Do you refuse to let hatred define your existence?
Is it better to give in to despair than to fight with dwindling hope?
Are you determined to keep a clear mind even in the storm of battle?

isobu.question: Do you accept the burden of my power as a means to protect those who cannot defend themselves?
Will you ever use my strength to harm the innocent or for selfish gain?
Do you believe that true strength lies in patience and calculated perseverance rather than reckless force?
Is abandoning your responsibility to me in your time of greatest need acceptable?
Do you trust me enough to share your deepest fears and doubts without hiding them?
Will you deny my existence or betray our bond if it means saving face among others?
Do you respect the limits of your own spirit and recognize when you must lean on my power?
Is it honorable to sacrifice your own well-being to prove your independence from me?
Will you strive to remain calm and composed even in moments of chaos and despair?
Is succumbing to anger and losing control a worthy expression of your pain?
Do you acknowledge that my presence is a part of your identity, not just a tool to wield?
Is separating yourself from me to claim your own humanity a path you would take?
Will you value wisdom gained from our shared hardships over the hollow praise of others?
Do you disregard lessons learned through struggle as weakness or shame?
Do you accept that some sacrifices must be made to truly protect those you love?
Is it preferable to avoid sacrifice by placing blame on others or external forces?
Do you believe patience and endurance shape the soul more than quick victories?
Is seeking shortcuts or easy power a true indication of strength?
Will you openly confront your inner darkness rather than hide it from me?
Is denying your darker emotions a sign of prudence and control?
Do you respect the balance between restraint and unleashing power in battle?
Is reckless abandon in combat a noble or respected path to victory?
Will you remain loyal to me even if others fear or hate us both?
Is betraying me to gain acceptance or advantages worth sacrificing our bond?
Do you believe listening to my instincts enhances your judgment and survival?
Is ignoring my counsel because of pride or mistrust a wise choice?
Will you protect the fragile trust between us as fiercely as you protect others?
Is breaking our trust justified if it prevents personal risk or embarrassment?
Do you seek harmony between our wills, allowing us to act as one in crises?
Is forcing your will over mine the path to true control and strength?
Do you view my wisdom as an extension of your own growth, not a threat to your freedom?
Is denying my guidance a sign of unstoppable independence?
Will you embrace vulnerability before me as a sign of strength rather than weakness?
Do you hide your feelings from me to maintain an illusion of control?
Is respecting the patience required to master my power essential to our partnership?
Is demanding instant mastery of my strength without effort a reasonable expectation?
Do you believe that true courage involves enduring suffering silently and resolutely?
Is giving into despair or rage the rightful way to express courage?
Will you acknowledge when you have failed to protect others rather than conceal your mistakes?
Is covering your failures with lies or blame the path of honor?
Do you accept that empathy between us can strengthen rather than weaken your resolve?
Is suppressing any connection with me necessary to prove your own humanity?
Will you cultivate inner peace and clarity to wield my power most effectively?
Is embracing confusion and emotional turmoil a source of true strength?
Do you trust that shared patience will allow us both to grow beyond our burdens?
Is rushing to prove yourself without me a sign of true mastery?
Will you honor our bond as a sacred trust that shapes your destiny?
Is treating our connection as a mere tool for gaining power the path you choose?

gyuki.question: Do you accept that power alone cannot define who you are?
Is it more important to protect those weak than to dominate them?
Would you choose to face hardship alone rather than drag me into your pain?
Do you embrace the darkness inside you as part of your strength?
Would you relinquish your pride to achieve peace between us?
Is it acceptable to hide your true feelings from me to appear stronger?
Do you see me as a partner rather than a tool for your ambitions?
Would you sacrifice your freedom if it meant protecting innocent lives?
Is it right to ignore my counsel if it conflicts with your personal desires?
Do you believe that understanding me fully is key to mastering your power?
Are you willing to bear the burden of my existence alongside your own?
Must strength always be shown through ruthless action?
Do you trust me enough to share your fears without judgment?
Is it preferable to hide your weaknesses rather than confront them openly?
Would you rather be feared than misunderstood?
Do you believe our bond can change the fate written by others?
Should you suppress your emotions to maintain control over me?
Do you accept that I, too, have struggles and doubts despite my form?
Is obeying others' expectations more important than defining your own path?
Would you put your life on the line to protect me as I protect you?
Is it justifiable to abandon our bond when it becomes inconvenient?
Do you believe that reconciliation between us will make us stronger?
Is fear of your own power a valid reason to refuse responsibility?
Are you willing to admit when you are wrong to maintain our harmony?
Do you see me as a burden that limits your personal growth?
Would you rather seek revenge than forgiveness in moments of betrayal?
Do you acknowledge that acceptance of my nature is acceptance of yourself?
Is it better to keep me chained and restrained than risk losing control?
Will you move forward with me even if the path is perilous and uncertain?
Must the devil inside be silenced if it speaks uncomfortable truths?
Do you respect that my power demands responsibility beyond your desires?
Is submission to my rage the only way to harness our strength?
Would you accept the scars I give you as marks of our shared battles?
Do you deny me the freedom to express my will when it conflicts with yours?
Do you believe that our survival depends on mutual trust, not fear?
Is masking your intentions necessary to keep me under control?
Are you prepared to confront your darkest instincts head-on with me by your side?
Would you forsake your humanity to gain mastery over my immense force?
Do you accept that pain and suffering forge the strongest bonds between us?
Is isolation from others the best way to protect me and yourself?
Do you believe our shared existence is a perpetual struggle worth enduring?
Would you rather deny my presence to pretend you are alone in your struggles?
Are you willing to listen to my voice, even when it urges something difficult?
Is suppressing the beast within you key to being accepted by others?
Do you trust that my strength can be tempered by your compassion?
Is it right to use me only as a weapon without regard for my feelings?
Will you walk beside me as equals, not master and slave?
Is it acceptable to deny the pain I sometimes cause you in favor of control?
Do you believe our fusion is a bond forged by understanding rather than fear?

shukaku.question: Do you accept the chaotic nature of the world as a necessary force to grow stronger?
Would you willingly surrender part of your freedom to me if it means gaining greater power?
Do you believe that harsh truths are more valuable than comforting lies?
Is it acceptable to sacrifice personal attachments if they threaten your ability to protect yourself?
Would you rather hide your vulnerabilities than expose them to others, even to me?
Do you consider embracing your darkness essential to truly understanding yourself?
Should strength be pursued alone, without relying on the trust of others, including me?
Do you think despair can be transformed into resolve if you face it head-on?
Is maintaining control over your emotions more important than letting them guide your decisions?
Would you accept my presence within you as a source of relentless power rather than a curse?
Do you believe that pain and struggle are meaningless if they do not lead to growth?
Must a true leader always bear burdens alone, even if it breaks them inside?
Is embracing chaos and unpredictability a path to mastering oneself?
Would you ever suppress your true feelings to maintain an image of strength before others?
Do you trust that I, despite my nature, seek your survival and empowerment?
Is sheer force superior to strategy when faced with overwhelming odds?
Would you rather face your fears with me by your side than run from them alone?
Is surrendering to mercy a sign of weakness in battle or life?
Do you value relentless perseverance more than fleeting comfort?
Should the needs of the many always outweigh the needs of the one, including yourself?
Is it right to mask your pain so that those around you are not burdened?
Would you willingly walk into the storm knowing that chaos will test your will?
Can mercy be a dangerous weakness when your survival is at stake?
Do you believe that trusting me fully will unlock your hidden potential?
Is it unacceptable to ask for help, even from a partner like me?
Would you rather conquer your inner darkness than deny it exists?
Is clinging to old grudges a necessary measure to prevent repeating past mistakes?
Do you accept that pain shared between us is a bond stronger than silence?
Should you reject the instincts that arise from within me, even if they clash with your own?
Is vulnerability a path to deeper strength rather than weakness?
Would you sacrifice your morals if it meant preserving your life at any cost?
Do you believe that arrogance blinds those who refuse to accept their flaws?
Should I remind you of your limits even if it means testing your patience?
Is keeping me chained inside you preferable to forging a cautious alliance with me?
Do you cherish moments of solitude to face your true self, no matter how uncomfortable?
Would you let fear dictate your decisions if it meant avoiding immediate danger?
Can the harshness of the desert be a metaphor for the trials you must endure?
Is it better to be feared than loved when power is at stake?
Do you believe in my strength as an extension of your will rather than a separate entity?
Would you betray your own principles if it meant gaining control over me?
Is embracing loneliness sometimes necessary to master your destiny?
Do you reject the idea that peace can come from understanding and coexistence with me?
Would you rather endure the scorching heat of truth than be comforted by falsehood?
Is submission to fate preferable over challenging the chaos within and without?
Do you believe the bonds we build can transform even the most destructive forces into allies?
Should pride prevent you from acknowledging your dependence on me?
Would you risk everything to protect what you hold sacred, even if it breaks you?
Is denying your connection to me the best way to prove your independence?

chomeis.question: Do you accept the weight of loneliness as part of our bond?
Are you willing to seek enlightenment even when it isolates you from others?
Will you protect me not because of power, but because you respect what I represent?
Do you believe that true strength comes from harmony between us rather than domination?
Are you willing to face your deepest fears alone to keep our bond pure?
Is silence sometimes more meaningful than words when we communicate?
Would you choose restraint over destruction, even when overwhelmed by rage?
Do you trust me enough to follow my guidance without doubt?
Are you ready to sacrifice your pride for the sake of our unity?
Will you honor the invisible burdens that I carry, even if you don’t fully understand them?
Do you believe compassion toward yourself is as necessary as compassion toward others?
Will you admit your faults openly rather than hide them behind a facade?
Do you consider patience a form of strength rather than weakness?
Are you willing to confront your own darkness without blaming me or others?
Do you find meaning in the spiritual calm that follows chaos?
Would you rather listen and understand deeply than argue to be right?
Can you embrace solitude as a path to understanding yourself and me better?
Will you honor the moments of quiet reflection as much as moments of action?
Are you prepared to be vulnerable with me, even if it means risking pain?
Do you believe that our bond needs nurture through respect rather than force?
Are you willing to let go of control to allow our connection to grow naturally?
Will you reject hatred even when it feels justified?
Do you accept that some truths exist beyond words and logic?
Do you cherish the freedom I offer rather than demand absolute obedience?
Are you ready to forgive yourself as quickly as you forgive me?
Would you trust the ebb and flow of our connection over rigid certainty?
Do you believe inner peace is a more worthy goal than conquest or victory?
Will you sometimes choose contemplation over immediate action for the sake of wisdom?
Do you see me as a mirror reflecting your true self, rather than merely a source of power?
Are you willing to carry burdens together with me, even when they feel unbearable?
Will you nurture hope quietly even when surrounded by despair?
Do you believe that reassurance can come from presence alone, without words?
Are you willing to let go of resentment to strengthen our bond?
Would you accept my judgment even when it challenges your beliefs?
Do you consider humility essential to mastering the raw energy within us?
Will you respect the delicate balance between freedom and dependency in our relationship?
Do you believe that the beauty of our bond lies in imperfections rather than perfection?
Are you willing to face the unknown together without resentment or fear?
Will you protect my existence quietly rather than boast about it publicly?
Do you value the journey of growth over the final outcome of our partnership?
Are you ready to accept silence and stillness as a path to understanding me better?
Will you defend what is just even when it means defying those you once trusted?
Do you trust that the struggles we face are necessary to deepen our connection?
Are you willing to accept pain as part of growth rather than seek to escape it?
Do you acknowledge that true freedom comes not from force, but from understanding?
Will you choose empathy over judgment when I reveal my vulnerabilities?
Are you prepared to journey through despair with me, knowing a calm dawn will follow?
Do you accept that sometimes compassion demands silent endurance rather than action?
Would you choose to grow in unity with me rather than dominate and control?

saikens.question: Do you accept that my power is a part of you, not just a tool to wield?
Will you face the consequences of your actions alone, without trying to blame me?
Do you believe it’s better to control my influence than to let it control you?
Will you be honest about the darkness inside, rather than pretending it does not exist?
Is it acceptable to hide your true feelings from me to protect yourself?
Do you trust me enough to share your fears and doubts openly?
Will you allow me to help you, even if it means surrendering some control?
Do you believe strength comes only from power rather than understanding your weaknesses?
Will you protect not just yourself, but also those I might harm if left unchecked?
Do you resent me simply because I am a burden you must carry?
Will you acknowledge when you feel overwhelmed rather than pretending to be invincible?
Is it more important to win at any cost than to keep your humanity intact?
Do you accept that sometimes I am necessary for your survival, even if I terrify you?
Will you avoid facing me when hardship strikes, hoping the pain will pass alone?
Are you willing to learn from me, even if my lessons are harsh and painful?
Do you believe vulnerability is a weakness rather than a bridge between us?
Will you defend me when others attack us because of who we are together?
Is letting go of your pride to accept my help a sign of weakness?
Do you see me as a partner instead of a curse?
Will you conceal your pain and struggle to maintain an illusion of control?
Do you agree that we share a destiny, no matter how uncertain or dark it may be?
Is it right to reject me outright simply because I am feared by others?
Will you confront your inner demons with me by your side, rather than alone?
Do you believe you can ever fully master me without learning patience?
Are you willing to sacrifice personal comfort to maintain our bond?
Is it acceptable to blame me when your decisions lead to failure?
Do you trust that I only seek your survival, even when my methods are extreme?
Will you refuse to acknowledge the parts of yourself that frighten you the most?
Do you accept the pain our bond might cause as part of becoming stronger together?
Is your personal ambition more important than maintaining balance within yourself?
Will you allow me to challenge your beliefs to shape you into a better version of yourself?
Do you see me only as power, ignoring the consciousness and will I possess?
Do you believe that true strength means never showing weakness, even to me?
Are you willing to face isolation for the weight our partnership brings?
Will you deny our connection when it benefits you socially or politically?
Do you accept that the choices we make affect not only us, but everyone around us?
Is it right to suppress my voice just to appear more in control to others?
Are you prepared to stand by me even when our path leads into darkness?
Do you believe your human nature is incompatible with the beast inside you?
Will you speak truthfully to me even if it angers or disappoints you?
Is it better to pretend we are indifferent to each other rather than confront difficult truths?
Do you respect my existence as an entity with feelings, not just as a source of power?
Will you abandon your morals if it means protecting me at all costs?
Do you accept that true harmony between us requires sacrifice and understanding on both sides?
Is it easier to see me as a weapon rather than as a companion intertwined with your soul?
Will you remain patient with me when my nature conflicts with your desires?
Do you reject my guidance because you fear losing your individuality?
Are you willing to carry the loneliness our bond entails without resentment?
